title: Graphene Lattice Symmetries Protect The Dirac Nodes
summary: Reflection and rotational symmetries of the honeycomb lattice keep the two graphene Dirac nodes pinned and at the same energy; only symmetry-breaking perturbations can turn them into masses and open the Haldane route to quantized Hall response.
assumptions: The starting model is the nearest-neighbor honeycomb Hamiltonian.
Perturbations preserve the lattice symmetries until the Haldane mass mechanism is discussed explicitly.
regime: Honeycomb-lattice symmetry analysis prior to opening a topological gap.
scope: Reusable theorem-level statement of why graphene has protected Dirac points before the Haldane mass is introduced.

source_anchors.notes: Witten's symmetry-based explanation of why the two Dirac nodes persist before the Haldane perturbation.
Original condensed-matter realization in which suitable symmetry breaking gaps the paired Dirac cones into a Chern insulator.

representation: Theorem-level symmetry statement for the graphene precursor of the Haldane model.

math_expressions.notes: Band-touching condition selecting the Dirac nodes.

symbols.meaning: graphene Bloch Hamiltonian near half filling
Brillouin-zone angles locating the two graphene Dirac points

failure_modes: This theorem by itself does not yet give the explicit Haldane mass term or the full lattice Chern-number calculation.
retrieval_hints: Use when the Haldane branch needs the explicit statement of what protects the ungapped graphene cones and what must be broken to gap them.
